title: Oscar Speech — Matthew McConaughey
description: McConaughey's heartfelt 2014 Oscar acceptance speech — natural American English at its best

sentences.text: First off, I want to thank God, because that's who I look up to.
He's graced my life with opportunities that I know are not of my hand or any other human hand.
He has shown me that it's a scientific fact that gratitude reciprocates.
In the words of the late Charlie Laughton, who said, 'When you've got God, you've got a friend. And that friend is you.'
To my family — dad, we'd sure be on the back nine today.
Miss you, man. Call me if you get a chance.
Mom, you really are my hero. And she's over there somewhere — there you go.
And my wife, Camila, and my kids Levi, Vida, and Livingston — the four people who get to tell me 'good morning' and 'goodnight' every day.
I love you all very much.
Now there are three things, to my count, that each of us needs each day.
Someone to look up to, something to look forward to, and someone to chase.
First off, to my hero. That's who I chase.
Now when I was 15 years old, I had a very important person in my life come to me and say, 'Who's your hero?'
And I said, 'I don't know, I've got to think about that. Give me a couple of weeks.'
I come back two weeks later, this person comes up and says, 'Who's your hero?' I said, 'I thought about it. You know who it is? It's me in ten years.'
So I turned 25. Ten years later, that same person comes to me and says, 'So, are you a hero?'
And I was like, 'Not even close. No, no, no.' She said, 'Why?' I said, 'Because my hero's me at 35.'
So you see every day, every week, every month and every year of my life, my hero's always ten years away.
I'm never gonna be my hero. I'm not gonna attain that. I know I'm not, and that's just fine with me because that keeps me with somebody to keep on chasing.
So, to any of us, whatever those things are, whatever it is we look up to, whatever it is we look forward to, and whoever it is we're chasing — to that I say, 'Alright, alright, alright.'
